include: CVE root cause is not primarily memory corruption.
The bug class can still survive a Rust rewrite: parser state bugs, arithmetic/limit bugs, infinite loops, decompression bombs, or data-leak/correctness issues.
CVE entries tied to C null-pointer crashes are kept only when they still imply parser invariant checks or state-transition tests for the Rust port.
exclude: Primary memory-corruption CVEs, even when triggered by arithmetic mistakes.
Bugs whose main lesson is C-specific ownership or longjmp cleanup rather than PNG parser behavior.
Standalone utility-only issues unless they directly change core library design priorities.

high_priority.issue_class: integer overflow in image/chunk size handling
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: Rust does not automatically prevent length or dimension arithmetic bugs. A naive port can still panic, reject valid input incorrectly, or mishandle allocation/limit checks if it uses unchecked casts or wraparound arithmetic.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Use checked arithmetic for row, chunk, and progressive-read size calculations and convert between integer types explicitly.

high_priority.issue_class: parser state not rolled back on chunk CRC error
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: This is a state-machine bug, not a buffer bug. Rust will not save the port if partially-updated decoder state survives an error path and later code assumes it is valid.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Chunk handlers should either validate before mutating state or restore invariants before returning an error. Add tests for malformed tRNS chunks with bad CRCs.
high_priority.local_source_note: Upstream CHANGES says the fix was to set num_trans to 0 before the error return in png_handle_tRNS.

high_priority.issue_class: transform logic bug from wrong operators/comparisons
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: Language safety does not prevent incorrect boolean/bitwise logic. Porting transform code mechanically can reproduce the same crash-inducing logic error.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Re-derive transform predicates from the PNG rules instead of line-by-line translation, and add property tests around palette and grayscale transforms.

high_priority.issue_class: incorrect handling of trailing bits in 1-bit interlaced rows
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: This is a data-sanitization and output-correctness bug. A safe implementation can still leak stale bits or produce wrong pixels if it fails to clear unused bits at row edges.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Mask or zero padding bits in packed rows, especially for 1-bit interlaced images whose widths are not divisible by 8.

high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: Rust does not prevent attacker-controlled CPU or memory amplification. Inflating ancillary chunks still needs explicit limits and early abort behavior.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Enforce hard caps on decompressed ancillary chunk size and compressed-to-uncompressed expansion before inflating.

high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: Arithmetic validity checks are still required in Rust. Malformed color-space metadata can still trigger panics or invalid math if zero/degenerate cases are not rejected.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Validate cHRM-derived denominators before use and test rgb_to_gray together with color-correction paths.
high_priority.local_source_note: Upstream CHANGES groups this with other rgb_to_gray and cHRM arithmetic fixes.

high_priority.issue_class: infinite loop on zero-length IDAT in progressive decode
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: An event-driven decoder can still spin forever if state transitions do not make progress on zero-length data. This is independent of memory safety.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Ensure the progressive reader advances or rejects a zero-length first IDAT chunk instead of remaining in the same processing mode.
high_priority.local_source_note: Upstream CHANGES says the fix was to move process_mode to PNG_READ_IDAT_MODE after recognizing IDAT.

high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: All PNG parsers still need explicit per-chunk and total-size policy enforcement. Memory-safe containers alone do not impose the right file-format limits.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Check every chunk length against configured user limits, and compute a separate upper bound for IDAT from image geometry plus deflate overhead.
high_priority.local_source_note: Upstream CHANGES first added user-limit checks for non-IDAT chunks, then a computed maximum for IDAT.

high_priority.issue_class: row_factor calculation overflow leading to divide-by-zero
high_priority.why_relevant_to_rust: This is another dimension-arithmetic bug in chunk-length validation. Rust still needs careful checked math and well-typed intermediate values.
high_priority.porting_takeaway: Derive row_factor with checked multiplication/addition and test extreme image geometry values against chunk-length validation logic.
high_priority.local_source_note: The local changelog/CHANGES entry describes this as a fix to the row_factor calculation in png_check_chunk_length.

secondary.issue_class: malformed iCCP handling caused null dereference
secondary.why_relevant_to_rust: The original crash used C null pointers, but the underlying lesson still applies: malformed ICC profile chunks must be rejected cleanly without leaving invalid parser state or triggering panics.
secondary.porting_takeaway: Treat malformed iCCP metadata as recoverable parse errors and keep ICC parsing/state updates behind validation barriers.

secondary.issue_class: palette invariant violation (empty or missing PLTE)
secondary.why_relevant_to_rust: A Rust port can still panic or miscompute if palette expansion assumes a non-empty palette without checking that the required PLTE state exists.
secondary.porting_takeaway: Make palette presence and minimum length explicit invariants before any palette-expansion transform runs.

secondary.issue_class: text-chunk state transition bug after remove and re-add
secondary.why_relevant_to_rust: The original symptom was a null dereference, but the real risk is inconsistent internal state across API mutations. A Rust write API can still get this wrong if remove/replace paths do not fully reestablish invariants.
secondary.porting_takeaway: Test load/remove/re-add sequences for text chunks and model chunk ownership so replacement leaves no half-cleared state behind.

deprioritized_or_excluded_examples.reason: Negative embedded profile length still deserves validation tests, but the CVE itself is mainly a C-style leak/cleanup bug that Rust Drop should mitigate.
Crafted tEXt memory leak is primarily an error-path cleanup issue rather than a durable parser design lesson for safe Rust.
Incomplete fix for the same negative-length cleanup leak family as CVE-2006-7244; useful as history, but lower priority for a Rust-first design.
Malformed sCAL chunk leak is another cleanup/resource-release bug whose primary mitigation comes from ownership-based cleanup.
This is mostly a C null-string calling-convention mistake rather than a PNG-format design issue; an idiomatic Rust API should avoid it entirely.
NVD calls out integer truncation, but upstream CHANGES describes the bug as a buffer overrun. It is therefore treated as memory-corruption, not part of this non-memory set.
The crash is tied to libpng's C error-handling and cleanup contract around png_read_image; a Result-based Rust API should not mirror that exact failure mode.
Disputed memory leak report in png_create_info_struct; weak signal for core Rust-port priorities.
The control-flow bug includes an infinite loop, but the assigned CVE is an out-of-bounds read; excluded here because the published issue is still primarily memory-safety.
